metadata.split_role: Expanded Suillus and Leccinum/scaber-stalk bolete records.

records.culinary_uses: Leccinum records are handled conservatively here; do not treat as casual edibles without expert-level ID and proper cooking.

mushroom_profile.summary: Gastroleccinum Scabrosum: check host tree needs field confirmation; Dry to slightly tacky; Whitish to grayish pores; and Scabers.
mushroom_profile.season_note: Usually encountered from July through September in the Upper Midwest / Great Lakes window.
research_notes: Needs source links and fuller field notes.

records.edibility_detail: Do not eat from this app entry. Use it for recognition and comparison only.
records.mushroom_card_note: Gastroleccinum Scabrosum: check host tree needs field confirmation; Dry to slightly tacky; Whitish to grayish pores; and Scabers.
records.field_identification: Check host association (host tree needs field confirmation), cap surface (Dry to slightly tacky), pore surface (Whitish to grayish pores), stem traits (Scabers), and staining reaction (Unknown). Treat this as a comparison/ID record until image and local confirmation improve.

records.notes: Ghost Bolete: pale Leccinum of wet/boggy birch-type places; pretty, but still a Leccinum caution record.

mushroom_profile.summary: Ghost Bolete: check Birch, Wet woods; Pale whitish cap; Whitish pores; and Scaber stalk.
research_notes: Needs source links and fuller field notes.
Very pale whitish cap, scaber-stalk Leccinum stem, wet/boggy habitat, and birch association are the important checks.

records.edibility_detail: Do not eat from this app entry. Use it for recognition and comparison only.
records.mushroom_card_note: Ghost Bolete: check Birch, Wet woods; Pale whitish cap; Whitish pores; and Scaber stalk.
records.field_identification: Check host association (Birch, Wet woods), cap surface (Pale whitish cap), pore surface (Whitish pores), stem traits (Scaber stalk), and staining reaction (Variable discoloration). Treat this as a comparison/ID record until image and local confirmation improve.

records.culinary_uses: Some Suillus are edible, but this record is treated as niche/cautious unless host and field marks line up cleanly.
records.notes: Grayish Suillus: Suillus-type bolete needing host-tree confirmation; keep as a cautious conifer associate.

mushroom_profile.summary: Grayish Suillus: check Conifers, Pine; Grayish to brownish Suillus cap; Yellowish pores; and Glandular dots possible.
research_notes: Needs source links and fuller field notes.
Check conifer host, cap stickiness/color, pore color, stem glandular dots, and whether ring/veil traces are present.
Suppressed from normal cards because no exact public-usable image is verified.

records.edibility_detail: Do not use this record as a food recommendation; treat it as comparison/recognition until reviewed with stronger local evidence.

Treat this as a comparison/ID record until image and local confirmation improve.
